Spring Whiplash, But Nicer for Now

Last Updated Mon May 04 at 2:49PM

Big swings are in store this week. After a chilly weekend, temperatures turn warmer through midweek with dry weather through Tuesday. We do have to account for a passing shower barely clipping the NC/VA border area this morning. A strong front moves through Thursday morning. Ahead of the front, a shower or thundershower is possible early Wednesday. Coverage ramps up late Wednesday and Wednesday night. Showers continue into Thursday morning, then taper off during the afternoon and evening. Temperatures tumble Thursday after the front passes, leading to a seasonably cold Thursday night.

It's the May the Fourth Be With You edition of the Monday Message, now available to our subscribers.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest

Voting in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest ended yesterday. We will announce the winners by the end of this week. Many thanks to everyone who submitted photos and voted in the contest. It's going to be another awesome calendar!

Get the free RWC Mobile App...: Apple and Android

Cloudy; Occasional showers and thunderstorms; Light West wind but breezy at higher elev's
Hi: 65
Thursday
Mostly cloudy; Early showers, (thunder possible) tapering off by afternoon, and more confined to the western escarpment; Blustery afternoon
Lo: 38
Thursday night
Mostly to partly cloudy; Much colder; Can't rule out a stray light evening shower along the western escarpment; Blustery
Hi: 61

Clearing skies; Cold morning; Cool afternoon
Lo: 43

Mostly clear; Chilly
Saturday

Subscribe

Forecast Discussion

We are in for big weather swings this week, from the weekend chill to a warmer first half of the week, then a strong front at midweek, followed by colder weather Thursday night and Friday.

Warm southwest flow is in place through midweek as high pressure shifts off the Atlantic Coast. That puts us on the warm side of the pattern through Wednesday. Weather is dry through Tuesday. A shower or thundershower is possible early Wednesday.

The main weather maker arrives late Wednesday into early Thursday as a cold front approaches from the west and strong southwest flow feeds moisture into the Southern Appalachians. Showers and thundershowers spread in from southwest to northeast late Wednesday and become widespread overnight. Rain continues into Thursday morning, then tapers from west to east during the afternoon as the front moves through. The western escarpment and Tennessee border dry out first, while areas along and east of the Blue Ridge hold onto rain longer, ending Thursday evening.

Temperatures follow the pattern. Wednesday is warm ahead of the front. Thursday starts mild but turns cooler after the front passes. Behind the system, colder and drier air settles in.

Friday through the weekend is dry. A weak front moves through Saturday night in northwest flow. The next front approaches from the northwest by Monday morning. Temperatures are cool Friday, somewhat milder Saturday, and a little warmer Sunday.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
